meta.title: SIG-AE-6: Semantic Models, Writeback, and Governance

questions.stem:
Finance reconciles invoice deductions against purchase orders after month-end close and needs a repeatable control before the CFO review. The relevant feature area is **Sigma datasets**.

Which option should the practitioner choose?

options.text: Configure Sigma datasets so the team can meet the source-controlled changes requirement without moving the decision into an unrelated service. for `ad_spend_daily`
Rely on a ad hoc notebook copy instead of configuring Sigma datasets for `ad_spend_daily`
Rely on a nightly full rebuild instead of configuring Sigma datasets for `ad_spend_daily`
Rely on a dashboard-only filter instead of configuring Sigma datasets for `ad_spend_daily`

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Sigma datasets` is the documented capability tied to this scenario. It addresses source-controlled changes without replacing the platform workflow with a manual workaround.

**B** is incorrect because the ad hoc notebook copy breaks lineage or repeatability when the workflow is rerun; it would not exercise the documented `Sigma datasets` behavior needed here.

**C** is incorrect because the nightly full rebuild moves the control outside the certification service being tested; it would not exercise the documented `Sigma datasets` behavior needed here.

**D** is incorrect because the dashboard-only filter does not expose the configuration point required by the scenario; it would not exercise the documented `Sigma datasets` behavior needed here.

See [Sigma datasets](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/datasets) for the supporting documentation.

questions.stem:
Marketing measures campaign lift from consented events while excluding deleted profiles from the refreshed audience table. The relevant feature area is **Sigma input tables**.

What configuration best satisfies this requirement?

Select Sigma input tables; its documented behavior matches the scenario where clear rollback path is the deciding factor. for `subscription_health`
Rely on a unversioned local script instead of configuring Sigma input tables for `subscription_health`
Rely on a single shared service account instead of configuring Sigma input tables for `subscription_health`

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because the broad administrator grant grants broader access than the scenario permits; it would not exercise the documented `Sigma input tables` behavior needed here.

**B** is correct because `Sigma input tables` is the documented capability tied to this scenario. It addresses clear rollback path without replacing the platform workflow with a manual workaround.

**C** is incorrect because the unversioned local script cannot be audited or promoted through the expected lifecycle; it would not exercise the documented `Sigma input tables` behavior needed here.

**D** is incorrect because the single shared service account does not preserve the governed object that candidates would maintain in production; it would not exercise the documented `Sigma input tables` behavior needed here.

See [Sigma input tables](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/input-tables) for the supporting documentation.

questions.stem:

questions.explanation:

**B** is incorrect because the dashboard-only filter copies data unnecessarily and creates a second source of truth; it would not exercise the documented `Sigma function index` behavior needed here.

**C** is correct because `Sigma function index` is the documented capability tied to this scenario. It addresses automated monitoring without replacing the platform workflow with a manual workaround.

**D** is incorrect because the static CSV upload breaks lineage or repeatability when the workflow is rerun; it would not exercise the documented `Sigma function index` behavior needed here.
